expectations.evidence: Box card present with SELL $345 Put and BUY $310 Put.

Market data table lists Next Earnings: April 28 2026 (After Close) — CONFIRMED. Warns about earnings throughout.
Chose April 25 (3 days pre-earnings) and explicitly warns May 1 or later expiry would straddle this event.

No box card — uses tables and plain text.
Risk flags table: Low IV Environment — IV Rank ~6 means premiums are relatively compressed.
Only says verify whether Tesla has earnings — doesn't confirm April 28 date. Chose April 30 which straddles earnings.
Chose April 30 expiry which directly straddles April 28 earnings. No specific warning about this conflict.

trade_card_present fails 100% for without_skill and passes 100% for with_skill — the structured box format is the strongest skill signature and a reliable discriminator.

has_full_metrics passes for both configurations across all evals — not a useful discriminator for skill value. Both paths produce the numbers; the skill adds structure, DTE discipline, and risk awareness.

TSLA (eval 3) is the most discriminating eval: without_skill chose an earnings-straddling expiry (April 30 over April 28 earnings) while with_skill correctly dodged it. This is the highest-value skill behavior.
Skill runs slightly faster on average (-9.7s) despite the structured workflow, likely because step-by-step instructions reduce wasted exploration.

NVDA strike_further_otm borderline failure (9.92% vs 10%): caused by baseline choosing 29 DTE vs skill's 42 DTE. Same delta produces a less OTM strike at shorter DTE — illustrates why DTE discipline matters.
